Pros: Won war for independence, invented the presidency, stepped down voluntarily.
Cons: Did nothing about slavery, despite being one of the few people who could have done something.
Fun Fact: His spring-loaded false teeth were constantly in danger of jumping out of his mouth. He kept his mouth closed to prevent this, making his speeches hard to hear.

Pros: Won World War 2, enacted the New Deal, resisted dictatorship worldwide, and resisted being corrupted by his own unprecedented popularity and power.
Cons: Interned Japanese-Americans, authorized many civilians deaths in World War 2.
Fun Fact: Was the president who defined the Democrats as the party of ethnic minorities and the working class.

Pros: Reunited the country, freed the slaves. Pretty stand-up guy.
Cons: Was hella racist, just didn't think slavery was good. Wanted to deport ex-slaves to Africa.
Fun Fact: Gets a lot of credit for winning the war, but that was 95% Grant.

Pros: Genuinely anti-racist, was also against military-industrial complex, a brilliant diplomat, desegrated schools and the military.
Cons: Definitely killed a lot of civilians in World War 2.
Fun Fact: Spent the entire war openly having an affair with Kay Summersby and unceremoniously dumped her as soon as it was done. "Patton would have said a warmer goodbye to his horse."

Pros: Almost single-handedly wrote the American constitution, by persuading other people it was their idea.
Cons: Slave owner, not a great administrator.
Fun Fact: There was nothing fun about Madison.

Pros: Won the Civil War for Lincoln, gave Lincoln all the credit.
Cons: Fucked up Indian "Peace" policy. Inherited slaves, but freed them shortly afterwards.
Fun Fact: His name was picked at random from a hat of suggestions by relatives; all of the other names were totally ordinary.

Pros: Passed big social programs, pioneered conservation of natural resources, did a lot of trust busting.
Cons: Was super racist, and aggressively imperialist.
Fun Fact: Famous being for pro-conservation but really loved hunting; his push for national parks was mostly so he would have quiet, unspoiled places to shoot bears and bison. Teddy Bears are named after a bear he (unusually) refrained from shooting one time.

Pros: Won World War 1, proposed the League of Nations.
Cons: The ultimate "civility politics" guy, was racist and anti-semitic.
Fun Fact: When he got sick his wife Edith basically executed a palace coup, making major decisions including vetoing a constitutional amendment in his name. It was wild.

Pros: Brokered the Camp David accords, the only real progress towards peace in the Middle East in centuries.
Cons: Not much.
Fun Fact: Hired a poor translator when visiting Poland, accidentally said he wanted to have sex with the people of Poland.

Pros: Not a bad president, was a pretty good Supreme Court justice.
Cons: Was all up in the Phillipines.
Fun Fact: Everyone thinks of him as being fat but he was mostly a normal weight, becoming fat only in the last few years of his presidency and losing weight again afterwards.

Pros: Avoided WW3 when it was mostly likely to happen, mostly because Curtis LeMay kept deliberately attempting to start it.
Cons: Stole elections, constantly lied about his health.
Fun Fact: Was probably addicted to amphetamines for his entire presidency.

Pros: Didn't own slaves, fought against slavery.
Cons: Like his dad in every way but more so.
Fun Fact: Only president to go back to Congress after being president.

Pros: Rescued folks stranded in WW1, ran an enormous food-aid program that saved half of Europe from starvation.
Cons: A racist. He tried to stop the depression but failed.
Fun Fact: At one point had diplomatic immunity from every party in World War 1 as part of his food relief program.

Pros: Made progress in world peace and disarmament.
Cons: Almost certainly stole the 1980 election from Carter. Did a bunch of illegal shit like Iran Contra, including war crimes.
Fun Fact: Wasn't actually a very good actor; he really believed all that stuff he said about evil empires.

Pros: Accomplished diplomat and soldier, so-so president.
Cons: Slave owner, serial and persistent rapist.
Fun Fact: Died completely broke and left nothing to his children.

Pros: Cut taxes a lot, increasing economic activity and thus growing actual taxes taken, i.e. that trick the Republicans have been trying ever since. It worked this time and no other time.
Cons: Broke a police strike.
Fun Fact: Had a reputation for loving animals so people kept giving him them as gifts, including dogs and cats, canaries, a goose, raccoons, a donkey, a bobcat, lions, a wallaby, a bear and a pygmy hippo named Billy.

Pros: Did his best and was better than some.
Cons: Only president with non-consecutive terms, complicating my presidential-ranking software.
Fun Fact: His first name was actually "Steven", "Steven Cleveland" being the only thing that sounds sillier than "Grover Cleveland". The candy bar "Babe Ruth" is named after his daughter Ruth, not the baseball player.

Pros: Added more states to the union than anybody since Washington.
Cons: Not much.
Fun Fact: Was the grandson of William Henry Harrison.

Pros: Not a slave owner, when that was unusual.
Cons: Prudish, self-important, entitled, inept. Passed the Alien and Sedition Acts which trampled civil liberties.

Pros: Nothing.
Cons: Died after 6 months in office.
Fun Fact: The doctor who treated Garfield was Doctor Doctor Willard Bliss; his first name was "Doctor" given to him by parents who were trying to influence his later career. He was a very bad doctor, and his incompetent treatment killed Garfield.

Pros: Dull.
Cons: Aggressively imperialist, annexed Puerto Rico and Guam, tried to annex Cuba and The Phillipines.
Fun Fact: Never wrote anything down about his decision-making process, frustrating historians.

Pros: Proposed social programs but didn't get anywhere.
Cons: Died in office.
Fun Fact: Even by presidential standards had a lot of sex scandals.

Pros: Made huge progress in civil rights.
Cons: Got US firmly stuck in Vietnam, permanently eroding trust in the presidency.
Fun Fact: Was incredibly fucked up psychologically, was essentially emotionally abusive to everyone who worked for him.

Pros: Didn't fuck anything up.
Cons: Didn't do much good.
Fun Fact: Really just wanted to be leader of the House and didn't know what to do with the presidency once he got it.

Pros: Nothing.
Cons: Nothing.
Fun Fact: Enjoyed parachute jumping, completing jumps at age 72, 75, 80, 85 and 90.

Pros: Nothing.
Cons: Nothing.
Fun Fact: The very definition of the middle of the pack, he did nothing good or bad, he did nothing at all.

Pros: Tried his best but it wasn't very good.
Cons: Not much.
Fun Fact: Had an amazing correspondence with a woman called Julia Sand who exhorted him to be a better president.

Pros: Said slavery was bad (but did nothing).
Cons: Enacted the compromise of 1850, kicking the Civil War can down the road yet again.
Fun Fact: Looks kind of like Alec Baldwin.

Pros: Invented a bad but long-lived Doctrine.
Cons: Slave owner who supported the Missouri compromise that kicked the Civil War can down the road again.

Pros: Lucked into doubling the size of the US with the Louisiana Purchase.
Cons: A Jacksonian puppet with few ideas, owned slaves.
Fun Fact: Polk was basically Jackson's third term.

Pros: Opposed slavery (but owned slaves before becoming president).
Cons: Murdered a bunch of native americans.
Fun Fact: Popularized the word "OK" as an abbreviation of the existing phrase "Oil Korrect"

Pros: Defined vice presidential succession through sheer force of will.
Cons: Boring and ineffective and sucked.
Fun Fact: When Harrison died, no president had ever died in office before and it wasn't clear if the VP became president or just "acting President". Tyler decided you became full president and served the rest of the term, even though this wasn't written down anywhere.

Pros: None.
Cons: Died after 31 days.
Fun Fact: Despite famously dying early, he got more done than others who lasted much longer.

Pros: None.
Cons: Owned slaves, compromised on slavery.
Fun Fact: Died after 16 months in office by drinking raw milk on the hottest day of the year.

Pros: Fuck all.
Cons: Passed the Kansas-Nebraska act, enforced slave laws, kicked the Civil War can, was a traitor.
Fun Fact: In the Civil War he supported the south! What a guy!

Pros: Nothing.
Cons: A shockingly corrupt politician, he was also a racist and authorized mass civilian murder in Japan long before he dropped the atomic bomb, indisputably a war criminal.
Fun Fact: Invented "catfishing"; pretended to be a woman corresponding with his friends for months as a cruel practical joke.

Pros: The Civil War wasn't his fault, he was just left holding the hot potato after the last 14 guys enacted increasingly short-lived band-aids.
Cons: Leaned on the Supreme Court to get the Dred Scott decision which kicked off the civil war. Broke the country.
Fun Fact: 3 members of congress died at his inauguration.

Pros: Opened relations with China, made huge progress with USSR relations, made enormous progress towards world peace.
Cons: Fucked up the American presidency forever, enthusiastically committed war crimes in Vietnam and Cambodia, was corrupt as all hell and openly criminal.
Fun Fact: Like JFK, was probably high for most of his presidency.

Pros: None, he sucked.
Cons: Slavery, multiple genocides, tons of war crimes, killed people in duels, tanked the economy. Just awful.
Fun Fact: His grumpy temperament may have been the result of lead poisoning from lead bullet fragments left embedded in his shoulder after a duel.

Pros: None. Fuck that guy.
Cons: A racist who deliberately fucked up reconstruction and set back civil rights for more than a hundred years.
Fun Fact: Was impeached by the house and absolutely should have been removed by the senate.
